title: Task: Propose the best placement for a non-authoritative Kernel `philosophy.md` and how it should be loaded. Read: - /Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md - /Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md - /Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md - /Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/README.md if useful - list of docs under /Users/...
summary_text: Best placement: - Use [kernel-philosophy.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/kernel-philosophy.md) Why: - It fits the loader model (`~/.claude/docs` is for on-demand, non-authoritative detail), so it stays out of the always-loaded authority layer. - It keeps `AGENTS.md`, `CODEX.md`, and `~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md` as the authoritative layer, while adding optional Kernel execution philosophy as supplemental context. Rejected alternatives: - [kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md) - Reason: this file is already the authoritative `/kernel` runtime contract; adding non-authoritative philosophy there blurs authority and changes the command contract surface. - [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md) - Reason: cross-workspace canonical instructions should stay at the governance core; adding Kernel-specific philosophy there risks conflicting precedence and reduces clarity. Load triggers (Kernel): - Load `[kernel-philosophy.md]` at startup when: - the task is orchestration-governance heavy (trade-off policy, lane selection rationale, authority boundaries, escalation criteria), - request is for “philosophy”, “principles”, “operating style”, “decision posture”, “risk appetite”, or similar, - /kernel or /vote is executed and the task context is non-trivial (not purely mechanical/code-only). - Do not load: - during `/kernel` smoke mode (`SMOKE_RESULT_MARKER` hard override), - for routine implementation/inspection tasks with low policy/architecture ambiguity, - for normal non-Kernel tasks. Precedence when loaded: - `AGENTS.md` > `CODEX.md` > `~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md` > `[kernel-philosophy.md]`. - If conflict occurs, defer to the higher layer and log the conflict for manual resolution.
